### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

### Parse XLSX Contents

Parses the incoming XLSX binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming lead records to a stable schema

Next: Load Rows into Notion

### Load Rows into Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty email

### Guard Non-Empty email

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Sort Leads Newest First

### Sort Leads Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

# Capture form submissions with Notion and Telegram

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture form submissions with Notion and Telegram. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual capture forms is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Download Source File → Parse XLSX Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Load Rows into Notion → Tag Pipeline Metadata → Guard Non-Empty email → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating capture forms in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Notion integration token; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your capture forms process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
